subject: Science
chapter: The World of Metals and Non-metals

activities_projects.title: Lustre Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify and distinguish between shiny (lustrous) and dull surfaces
steps: Observe the surface of each material as it is
Rub each material with sandpaper to remove any dust or rust
Observe which materials shine after being cleaned
Classify them into 'Lustrous' and 'Non-lustrous'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that metals generally have a shiny appearance (metallic lustre)
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of materials based on lustre (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Malleability Test
activities_projects.objective: Observe how different materials react to being hammered
steps: Place the iron nail on a hard surface and hit it carefully with a hammer
Repeat the process with aluminium wire, pencil lead, and coal
Observe if the material flattens into a sheet or breaks into pieces
Identify which substances are malleable and which are brittle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between malleability in metals and brittleness in non-metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in identifying malleable vs. brittle substances (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ductility Detective
activities_projects.objective: Explore the property of materials being drawn into wires
steps: Try to gently stretch the materials
Look around the house for items that are shaped like wires
List which materials are used for making electrical wires and why
Explain why we don't see wires made of coal or sulfur
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands ductility as a characteristic property of metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation of material choice for wires (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Ringing Test (Sonority)
activities_projects.objective: Test which materials produce a ringing sound when struck
steps: Strike the metal spoon against a hard surface
Strike the wooden block and the plastic scale similarly
Listen to the sound produced (ringing vs. thud)
Relate this to why school bells are made of metal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies sonority as a property of metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of sonorous materials (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Heat Conductor Race
activities_projects.objective: Compare heat conduction in metals and non-metals
steps: Place both spoons in a glass of hot water
Wait for 2 minutes
Touch the handle of both spoons
Note which one feels hotter
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that metals are good conductors of heat
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate recording of temperature difference (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simple Electric Tester
activities_projects.objective: Test the electrical conductivity of various objects
steps: Connect the battery and bulb to make an open circuit
Place a coin in the gap and see if the bulb glows
Repeat with an eraser and the graphite of a pencil
Record which materials allow electricity to pass
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies conductors and insulators among common objects
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful circuit testing and data recording (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rusting Observation
activities_projects.objective: Identify the conditions required for iron to rust
steps: Jar 1: Place a dry nail (Air only)
Jar 2: Place a nail completely in boiled water with a layer of oil on top (Water only)
Jar 3: Place a nail half-dipped in water (Air and Water)
Observe for 3 days and see which nail develops a brown coating
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that both air and moisture are needed for rusting
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of rusting conditions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hardness Scale
activities_projects.objective: Compare the hardness of different elements
steps: Try to scratch the soap with your fingernail
Try to scratch the iron nail with your fingernail
Try to cut the candle wax with a plastic knife
Rank the materials from softest to hardest
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that most metals are hard but some substances are soft
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical ranking of materials by hardness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Uses of Non-metals Poster
activities_projects.objective: Research and display the importance of non-metals in life
steps: Identify non-metals like Oxygen, Nitrogen, and Chlorine
Find out one use for each (e.g., breathing, fertilizers, water purification)
Draw or paste pictures representing these uses
Label them clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the vital role of non-metals in everyday life
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and correctness of information (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metal vs. Non-metal Bingo
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce vocabulary and properties of elements
steps: Create a 3x3 grid with names of metals and non-metals
Teacher/Friend calls out a property (e.g., 'I am liquid at room temperature')
Cross out the corresponding element (e.g., Mercury)
The first to get a row/column wins
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quick recall of elemental properties and names
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and correct identification during the game (10 marks)
